scenario: Granite Banking runs Camunda 8.8 across 6 Spring Boot services and uses OIDC to obtain access tokens for its 18000 jobs per day. The platform team has not set camunda.client.auth.connect-timeout in any application.yaml and now needs the documented out-of-the-box value to size its OAuth provider readiness probes.
question: What is the documented default value of camunda.client.auth.connect-timeout in the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter?

options.text: The properties reference lists PT5S as the default connect-timeout for outbound calls to the OAuth credentials provider in 8.8 services.
The properties reference lists PT1S as the default connect-timeout for outbound calls to the OAuth credentials provider in 8.8 services.
The properties reference lists PT30S as the default connect-timeout for outbound calls to the OAuth credentials provider in 8.8 services.
The properties reference lists null as the default connect-timeout, requiring an explicit value before the OAuth client can issue requests.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.auth section of the 8.8 properties reference lists PT5S as the default duration for connect-timeout against the OAuth credentials provider.

b.text: Incorrect. PT1S is not the documented default; the reference table records PT5S as the default connect-timeout for OAuth credentials provider calls.

c.text: Incorrect. PT30S is not the documented default; the reference table records PT5S as the default connect-timeout for OAuth credentials provider calls.

d.text: Incorrect. The reference lists an explicit PT5S default; null is the default only for properties without a baked-in duration.

explanation: The camunda.client.auth properties reference table lists PT5S as the default for connect-timeout, the connection timeout used for requests to the OAuth credentials provider.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
